Off Calendar Jun-15-2009 Continued To Apr-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PTR-06-288402 (The Thomas J. Collier Trust).

Appearances: Edward Koplowitz for Timothy Jones (Former Trustee). M. Armon Cooper for Robert Hobson (Trustee).

The Court stated that the Estate action was trailing the Trust. In regard to discovery, Mr. Cooper stated that they were working on a report; a damage study/experts report, that the report should be ready in two weeks, and that he and Mr. Koplowitz had not talked yet. Mr. Koplowitz stated that in the last two months he hadn't received anything and that he's powerless at this point. The Court stated that the wait was over and referred the matter to Department 206, the Presiding Judge, for trial setting in case number PTR-06-288402. Discussions regarding the Estate matter were held. Mr. Cooper stated that the Estate was closed and the Court stated that an accounting was still pending. Status on the Estate matter, case number PES-01-282606, was set for April 19, 2010. Parties were advised to notify the Examiner if the Estate matter is taken care of.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy S. McDaniels.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case no. 288402.

Appearances: M. Armon Cooper for Successor Trustee Debra Dolch. Ed Koplowitz for the Former Trustee.

Mr. Cooper stated that all discovery requests are opposed by Mr. Koplowitz, that Mr. Koplowitz is holding Wells Fargo Records, and that he is waiting for a response from Mr. Koplowitz as to the records he has reviewed. Mr. Koplowitz stated that this case is moving backwards, that they are not getting a resolution, and that he is reviewing microfilm of 3000 documents. Mr. Cooper stated that there are records showing Jones used their money, that they believe there is over a million dollars and attorney's fees that are owed to them, and that they discovered this through their own discovery records. In addition, Mr. Cooper addressed the reason for the delay as set forth on the record. Mr. Koplowitz responded. The Court suggested counsel sit down and talk since the landscape is different and a few years had passed. The matters were continued to April 13, 2009.
